ab: kakakedriyan aken i kuskus a niatapaw kapatagilj anga ken a magakku patjavat anga matawing i lizulizuk a nia sikacu.
ch: 小時候我們家住在高士，上學以後，就搬到了桃園，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in kuskus. After I started school, we moved to Taoyuan and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: ka madaigaku anga ken kitulu aken ta kinacalisiyan a bungka sa ku keljang anga ta i taiwan a kacalisiyan izua ngaquaqn namakakaizua a bunka.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: manasika tucu a cavilj nu kaljapulavayan uri vaik aken kati kaka tjavulung a uqaljai a paljilji ta ramaljemaljeng a uri kiljaljuai ta nimitja a kemacidiljan a bunka.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: alu anga puluq a cavilj na ramaljemaljeng ljakua nu tja pacunan namaya ta inika vuluvulung.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: ku kivadaq timadju pida su kinacaqu a kai macaqu aravac a ikai ta pinaiwanan saigu uta ikai ta na tjukuku zipun kilang a kai.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的排灣語說得很流利，也會說中文、客家話和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in paiwan, as well as Chinese, Hakka and Japanese.

ab: ka kivadaq anga ken tjaimadju kemeljang anga aken tu na kemasi kakedriyan kitulu ta zipun a kai a valjaw nimadju pairang i tua karakudakudain nimadju tjuruvu a kineljang a kilang manasika liyaw a kinacaqu a kai.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是平地人，而他工作上認識許多客家人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Chinese, and he knows many Hakkas at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: tazua kakiqaqivuavuan amen liyaw a sini pasemalaw a pakatua qemaljup a saiguan kata kakudan.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: kumalji inika liyaw a niakeljange ljakua nia pinaka tua dinguwa a paiga maqati a gemalju kiljaluai a kilangeda.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: kakakedryan aken neka nu ku kemeljang tua ku bunka masiyaq aken a maciljilj ta ku kinacalisiyanan.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: nu cuai anga nu tjaliau a kineljangan paka pakisumalji a kinanguaq kinabulai sedjalep a sikizengel a kinapaiwanan.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為排灣族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Paiwan is unique and something to be proud of.
